steps.why: The IndexDir implementation shows a runtime table exposed as an immutable directory.
The examples recipe exercises table-backed feature-to-specimen lookup.
The backend row owns reusable Table and Dir implementations.
The mounted namespace row composes independently owned backends without copying their storage behavior.
The emitter row keeps generated storage contract evidence current.
The backend-composition recipe exercises the mounted Table/Dir surface.

title: Add a Table or Dir backend

steps.why: Start with the shared codec contract and position rules.
The emitter row keeps generated codec contract evidence current.

The FEMM row owns mesh, field, material, solve, and ODE helpers.
The linear solver row owns solver contracts, factor handles, iterative kernels, and certificate evidence.
The checked implementation specimen proves solver reuse metadata and residual evidence for certificates.
The discrete stack owns combinatorics and rank integration.
The finite enumeration row owns lazy words, digit/radix adapters, cyclic canonicalization, and longest-only selection.
The xtask documentation row owns generated doc lanes and index fragments.
The host row owns placement, device adapters, and hardware-facing stream surfaces.
The exec row keeps host process behavior in a loadable library.
Host primitives start from shared network and surface-card helpers.
The host storage row points to file, database, and HTTP backends.
The index core row owns graph ids, records, edges, and validation.
The kernel contract row owns the reusable protocol boundary plus bare Cx, eager Cx, and plain test context helpers.
The emitter keeps generated contract evidence current.
